Last week, I sat with 25 data & analytics leaders from various enterprises to discuss their most pressing questions and reflections around GenerativeAI. These leaders came from the Automotive, Chemical, Higher Education, Financial Services, Insurance, Healthcare, Manufacturing, Retail, & Technology industries (to name a few). Below are some of my takeaways:
...
-The Chief AI Officer (CAIO) role isn't for every enterprise. The necessity for a CAIO depends on
...
organizational structure and current infrastructure. In
...
, a centralized AI leader
...
, and roles like a
...
Director for AI under a Chief Data Officer (CDO) might be more appropriate.
...
-Organizations are establishing GenAI champions to drive AI adoption. These champions bridge the gap between technical teams and business stakeholders, ensuring strategic alignment and effective utilization of AI capabilities.
...
-Concerns include the potential of perceived obsolescence of data scientists, the need for explainable AI in regulated sectors, and the responsible deployment of AI capabilities to non-analytics users.
...
-Governance is crucial in tool selection and deployment, especially concerning proprietary versus commercial AI solutions.
...
-Securing funding for GenAI initiatives is
...
but crucial for success
...
with the C-suite, defining a centralized strategy, and prioritizing initiatives are key steps.
...
-GenAI initiatives spark a renewed interest in data quality and literacy, but don't inherently solve for either. GenAI has highlighted the importance of analytics and fostered curiosity among stakeholders who may have previously been disinterested.
...
Topics for future discussion include developing policies for tool usage, potential outcomes of sandboxing, and taking a deeper look at AI deployment. We will be reconvening on March 19th - let me know if you would like to join the conversation.

results.title: Adam Kelloway
highlights:
Artificial Intelligence Technical Leader at The Dow Chemical Company
...
This all started with wastewater! Ever since I was first introduced to the power and beauty of computer aided problem solving when I was given the task of translating bacterial bio-chemical reactions into ones and zeros at Cargill I have been hooked. A passion for solving computer aided problems was born and naturally evolved to a Ph.D. where I focused on the technical and economic optimization and analysis of biorefineries. Today, the focus is not bacteria or biorefineries but world-scale supply chains for Dow. The same rules hold true, focus on asking the right questions, solving the right problems, and keeping the economic incentive front and center. Technically, I have added to my MIP skills developed during my Ph.D. with additional capabilities in ML, DL, and RL model development. I work as part of a full-stack agile scrum team to build and deploy complete end-to-end solutions which deliver value for internal partners. All in all, I love to solve problems that drive value for companies using mathematics. Bringing AI to a legacy global manufacturer has not always been a smooth ride (no surprise there!) but this has allowed me to grow tremendously and now I am a leading part of the team responsible for defining the AI Strategy for Dow. Outside of work I tend to find myself coding still - be it home automation, tinkering with self-driving robots (Jetbot) or the occasional kaggle competition but also find myself being the family IT/tech guy - a role I actually enjoy playing. I am also passionate about inspiring the next generation and have volunteered for STEM outreach efforts and mentor University of Minnesota graduate students. Skills/Experience/Interest: Mixed Integer Programming, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Reinforcement Learning, Python, Gurobi, scikit-learn, Tensorflow, PyTorch, Ray (RLLib, Ray Tune, Ray Serve), Azure ecosystem, Agile Development.
...
[The Dow Chemical Company](
...
#### Artificial Intelligence Technical Lead
...
Member of Dow’s Digital Fulfillment Center (DFC). Responsible for working with Dow’s businesses to identify, shape, and deliver innovations that accelerate Dow’s digital supply chain vision. Part of a full stack agile scrum team to develop and deploy end-to-end solutions. My role on this team is to shape, build, and deploy machine learning and mixed integer optimization models and manage partnerships with other Dow model development teams. Leading the establishment of the AI/ML Strategy for Dow with emphasis in DataOps, MLOps, & Modeling thought leadership and evangelizing value generating potential of these technologies. Defining and driving best practices for scalable and sustainable model development and deployment in Azure. Projects (total value: multiple tens millions dollars) Reinforcement Learning for Supply Chain Planning Mixed Integer Programming for Supply Chain Planning and Scheduling Transport Carbon Footprint tracker International Trade Code Classification Managing and participating in University research collaborations Managing and participating in vendor research collaborations Technologies Ray ecosystem: Ray Tune, RLLib, Ray Serve Gurobi for MIP scikit-learn, tensorflow, pytorch Azure ecosystem: AML, ADF, AKS, Function Apps, Blob storage. Python, SQL Recognitions: Represent Dow on Manufacturing Leadership Council 2021 & 2020 Nominee for Integrated Supply Excellence award (internal global award)

This event is an incredible opportunity for Dow's Data Science community to share ideas and progress. I look forward to it every time, and this year, as usual, was a great success! Wow! What a great couple of days in Dow's HQ (Fellows Hall) at our biannual Data Science Challenge & Conference, hosted by Dow's Insight Scientist Community. Incredible innovations being explored focused on how we improve growth in our businesses and explore monetizing digital and AI capabilities that can help Dow generate additional revenue streams. Huge shout out to Satya Amaran , Anna Zink , Sophie Kim , Jim Peters and Michael Hausinger for all their efforts in hosting this amazing event. 
 
 The challenge encourages our employees to pitch Digital & AI capabilities from all our functions & businesses in front of sharks for bragging rights, future sponsorship and prioritization to assist in deploying their idea/solution.
 
 The event brought in external speakers, in particular Victor M Zavala who provided a very relevant topic on "Transforming Data to Decisions using Geometry, Optimization and Machine Learning". Thank you Victor for traveling in to join us for the last 2 days. 
 
 Another exciting part of this event is the cross functional & business poster session that highlights some of the great work happening at our company over the past year that is generating significant value through Analytics & AI enablement. And the seminars hosted by individual employees that explore in depth some of our breakthrough AI/ML & Data Science technology along with innovative solutions.
 
 And finally, a huge thank you to our sponsors Chris Bruman , Daniel Futter , Melanie Kalmar ... we truly appreciate your support and look forward to seeing these ideas/solutions enable improved growth & monetization for the company.

highlights:
Senior Staff Generative AI Scientist | Wfh
...
# Senior Staff Generative AI Scientist
...
Posted Jun 30, 2026
...
The Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI will apply knowledge and experience to real world problems and seek to utilize their skills reduce the cost of healthcare and improve health quality and outcomes. With access to dedicated on premise and cloud based big data solutions, the team can work with a vast amount of structured and unstructured data including claims, membership, physician demographics, medical records and others to begin to solve some of the most pressing healthcare issues of our time. A Data Scientist at Cotiviti will be given the opportunity to work directly with a team of healthcare professionals including analysts, clinicians, coding specialists, auditors and innovators to set aggressive goals and execute on them with the team. This is for an ambitious technologist, with the flexibility and personal drive to succeed in a dynamic environment where they are judged based on their direct impact to business outcomes.
...
- As a Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI within Cotiviti you will be responsible for delivering solutions that help our clients identify payment integrity issues, reduce the cost of healthcare processes, or improve the quality of healthcare outcomes. You will work as part of a team and will be individually responsible for the delivery of value associated with your projects. You will be expected to follow processes and practices that allow your models to be incorporated into our machine learning platform for production execution and monitoring, however, initial exploratory data analysis allows for more flexible experimentation to discover solutions to the business problems presented.
...
- Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Operations, along with product management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques.
...
- Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases.
...
- Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work.
...
- Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA.
...
- Work with production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance.
...
- Participate in other data science teams collaborating with your peers to support their projects.
...
- Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team.
...
- Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform.
...
- Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption.
...
- PhD or Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI.
...
- Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases.
...
- 9+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU.
...
- Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP.
...
- Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets.
...
- Passion for collaboration, learn it all mindset and driving value with AI.
...
Date of posting: 1/14/2026

Lead AI/ML solutions for General Motors' Product Safety Data Analytics team, developing end-to-end machine learning pipelines including generative AI and agentic AI systems in a hybrid role at the Warren, Michigan technical center.
...
## Job description
...
Our Product Safety Data Analytics team is seeking an experienced AI/ML Data Scientist with hands-on experience in the full end to end data science lifecycle for artificial intelligence & machine learning.
...
This role requires programming and statistical techniques to solve complex problems and work within a team framework on new AI/ML solutions.
...
As an AI/ML Data Scientist, you will be responsible for working with our customers to understand their challenges and needs, develop new machine learning solutions, work with ML Ops in a cloud environment, develop proof of concepts for new generative AI solutions and Agentic AI solutions.
**What You’ll Do**
...
* Work with our existing business partners to identify opportunities for new AI/ML solutions to enhance processes and enable our team members to be more efficient
...
* Train new machine learning models to solve complex business problems.
...
* Prototype new AI solutions, including Generative AI, to solve business problems.
...
* Develop new Agentic AI solutions to streamline business process
...
* Ehnance existing model solutions as business and technology continues to evolve
...
* Provide guidance on business problems using statistical methods and can craft ad-hoc reports to share findings and recommendations with business partners.
...
* Build statistical models that depict company-wide trends.
...
* Perform testing and validation of data sets.
...
refine storytelling skills
...
**Your Skills & Abilities (Required Qualifications)**
...
* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field
...
* 5+ years of experience in machine learning, engineering, data science, or a related field
* Looking for experience in the following:
...
* **Programming & Frameworks**: Python, R, Java, PySpark, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, LangChain, SQL
...
* **Machine Learning & AI**: Large Language Models (LLMs), Generative AI, RAG, Generative AI, Deep learning, Reinforcement Learning, Natural Language Processing (NLP), SVM, XGBoost, Random Forest, Decision Trees, Clustering
...
* **Data Engineering**: Databricks, Hadoop, SQL, Data Pipelines, Data Preprocessing & Feature Engineering
...
* **Cloud & Big Data Platforms: (**Preferred Microsoft Azure (Data Lake, Machine Learning, Databricks), **Nice to Have** (AWS (S3, SageMaker, Bedrock) or Google Cloud Platform (BigQuery, Dataflow, AI Platform) )
...
* **Deployment & MLOps:** **MLflow**, Model Monitoring & Versioning, Docker & Kubernetes, **GitHub**, Jira
...
* **Data Analysis & Visualization**: Tableau, PowerBI, Pandas, NumPy
...
* Proven track record delivering AI/ML solutions to customers that get continued usage and drive value
...
**What Will Give You A Competitive Edge (Preferred qualifications)**
...
* Deep knowledge of GM’s Data Ecosystem
* Deep knowledge of GM’s Cloud Technology Stack for Data Science
...
* Deep knowledge of Global Product Safety’s SFI Process
...
* Extensive NLP solutions from business problem statement to deployment and ongoing optimization
...
* Expertise with Large Language Models solutions from business problem statement to cloud deployment that have provided significant incremental business value
...
* Experience with generative AI solutions that you have developed and deployed into a production environment that have provided significant incremental business value
